response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a technically accomplished individual contributor with 15+ years of software development experience, genuine developer tooling credentials (test runner, assertion library, CI/CD setup), and strong polyglot engineering depth. However, the role is explicitly a people-leadership position requiring at minimum 2 years of people management and 3 years of technical leadership — neither of which is evidenced anywhere in the resume. His leadership exposure is limited to founding a side project and holding "Staff" IC titles. Without any demonstrated experience managing engineers, running 1:1s, setting performance expectations, or leading org-level initiatives, he does not meet the foundational requirements for this management role.
strengths: Genuine developer tooling experience: built an open-source test runner (Trial), a TDD assertion library (Fluent-Asserts), and set up CI/CD infrastructure at Altom — directly relevant to the engineering productivity focus
Extensive breadth across stack, platforms, and languages demonstrates the technical credibility required to guide system design and review engineering work across a diverse team
Community engagement through workshops, conference talks, and Code Kata sessions shows an inclination toward knowledge-sharing and mentorship, which is a precursor to effective engineering management
concerns: No people management experience whatsoever — the job requires a minimum of 2 years managing direct reports, and there is zero evidence of this anywhere in the resume
No organizational change leadership — the preferred profile asks for 3 years leading org-level change; the candidate has operated exclusively as an IC throughout his career
Mobile platform knowledge is superficial — a single stint maintaining an iOS/Apple TV app does not constitute the depth expected for a manager overseeing a large mobile platform ecosystem

key_factors.factor: No technical leadership at organizational scale
